data.title: US natural gas futures dip 1% as mild March curbs demand
data.description:
Key points:
  • Mild weather reduces US natural gas demand, affecting futures prices
  • Pipeline constraints keep Waha Hub prices negative for 26 days
  • US LNG exports decrease despite global price surge due to Iran conflict

By Scott DiSavino

U.S. natural gas futures eased about 1% on Friday on forecasts for less demand next week than previously expected and mild weather through the rest of March, which should keep heating demand low and allow utilities to add gas into storage in coming weeks.

Front-month gas futures for April delivery on the New York Mercantile Exchange fell 4.0 cents, or 1.2%, to $3.193 per million British thermal units (mmBtu). On Thursday, the contract closed at its highest since February 13 for a second day in a row.

For the week, the front-month was down about 1% after gaining about 11% last week.

In the U.S. cash market, average prices at the Waha Hub (NG-WAH-WTX-SNL) in West Texas remained in negative territory for a record 26 days in a row as pipeline constraints trapped gas in the Permian, the nation's biggest oil-producing shale basin.

SUPPLY AND DEMAND

Average gas output in the U.S. Lower 48 states rose to 109.8 billion cubic feet per day (bcfd) so far in March, up from 109.2 bcfd in February, according to data from financial firm LSEG. That compares with a monthly record high of 110.6 bcfd in December 2025.

Energy analysts said mostly mild weather in recent weeks has allowed energy firms to take the somewhat unusual step of injecting gas into storage during the winter heating season in March, boosting stockpiles to near normal levels for the week ended March 13, up from around 1% below normal for the week ended March 6.

Meteorologists forecast weather across the country will remain mostly near normal through March 28 with colder weather expected next week than this week. Next week's cold will likely result in an increase in heating demand, forcing some utilities to again pull gas from storage.

LSEG projected average gas demand in the Lower 48 states, including exports, would jump from 112.7 bcfd this week to 124.6 bcfd next week before dropping to 116.8 bcfd in two weeks. The forecast for next week was lower than LSEG's outlook on Thursday.

LNG EXPORTS

Average gas flows to the nine big U.S. liquefied natural gas (LNG) export plants slid to 18.3 bcfd so far in March, down from a record 18.7 bcfd in February.

Prices in the U.S. have reacted to the Iran war, but not by nearly as much as elsewhere in the world because America produces all the gas it needs and was already exporting all the LNG it could before the war. So, no matter how high global gas prices go, the U.S. cannot export much more gas.

Since the U.S. and Israel started bombing Iran on February 28, U.S. gas prices have climbed about 14% versus around 53% in Europe (TRNLTTFMc1) and 50% in Asia (JKMc1).

Around the world, gas was trading near $17 per mmBtu at the Dutch Title Transfer Facility (TTF) (TRNLTTFMc1) benchmark in Europe and $16 at the Japan-Korea Marker (JKM) (JKMc1) benchmark in Asia.

data.description:
Nym will hold a community call on March 13 at 15:00 UTC to celebrate one year of NYMVPN. According to the announcement, the session will include team members and a giveaway of free passes.

Refer to the official tweet by NYM:

Nym
@nym

Come by the NYM Community call tomorrow as we celebrate ONE YEAR NYMVPN!🥳

We will be giving away free passes and talking to members of the team!

📅March 13th

â²ï¸1500 UTC

SET REMINDERâ¤µï¸https://t.co/hky7StxGWN pic.twitter.com/Fm1F1ITlcx

Mar 12, 2026

NYM Info

Users employ NYM tokens to gain access to the mixnet, consequently improving the privacy of their data. NYM is also used as a reward for mix nodes in exchange for their services in traffic mixing and privacy assurance for Nym network users.

Fees accumulated in NYM are directed to a reward pool and later distributed amongst mix nodes, based on their performance and reputation. Each mix node's reputation is quantified in NYM tokens.

Moreover, users have the option to delegate their NYM tokens to mix nodes. This action serves as a validation of a mix node's reputation and grants the user a portion of the mix node's rewards. Users are encouraged to delegate their NYM to nodes that consistently deliver quality services as it increases their likelihood of earning rewards.

However, it should be noted that NYM is not a general-purpose payment mechanism or a typical cryptocurrency, such as Bitcoin. Its utilization is exclusively intended for activities within the Nym network.

data.title: PCE inflation report meets expectations, GDP is soft; markets rally
data.description:
The U.S. Commerce Department said on Friday its Personal Consumption Expenditures Price Index (PCE) rose largely in line with expectations in January, the latest sign that inflation remained relatively well behaved ahead of the war with Iran that began last month.

Friday's PCE Index rose 0.3% from December, compared with the 0.3% estimate of economists polled by Reuters and the prior 0.4% rise in December. Stripping out the volatile food and energy components, the so-called core PCE Price Index increased 0.4% last month, in line with expectations.

In the 12 months through January, PCE inflation increased 2.8%, compared with expectations for a 2.9% rise. The core PCE Price Index increased 3.1% last month. That was in line with estimates and followed a revised 3% rise in the core inflation in December. The Federal Reserve tracks the PCE price measures for its 2% inflation target.

Meanwhile, the Commerce Department's second estimate showed gross domestic product (GDP) increased by 0.7% in the fourth quarter, compared with 1.4% growth expected by economists polled by Reuters.

MARKET REACTION:

STOCKS: U.S. stocks rose following the data's release, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average , S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite rising 0.6%.

BONDS: U.S. Treasury yields slipped. The 10-year yield was last down 3 basis points at 4.24%. The two-year yield (US2YT=RR), which is sensitive to expectations for Fed policy, was down 6 basis points to 3.70%.

FOREX: The dollar index rose 0.3% to 100.32.

COMMENTS:

JAMES ST. AUBIN, CHIEF INVESTMENT OFFICER, OCEAN PARK ASSET MANAGEMENT, SANTA MONICA, CALIFORNIA:

"The Fed’s preferred inflation measure is still running hot thanks to services. It certainly doesn’t help the dovish case, but the reality is it’s old news. The effects of skyrocketing energy prices are just starting. If you’re looking for a silver lining it’s that goods prices remain somewhat contained."

MATT BUSH, U.S. ECONOMIST, GUGGENHEIM INVESTMENTS, NEW YORK:

“The big news is the core PCE inflation number coming in not quite as bad as feared. We've had relatively good news on the CPI inflation front in recent months, but core PCE inflation has been quite a bit hotter than the CPI data. And while that was still true with January's numbers, the January core PCE wasn't quite as bad as feared. And so I think that's causing some reaction in rates markets and pricing for the path of Fed policy.”

PETER CARDILLO, CHIEF MARKET ECONOMIST, SPARTAN CAPITAL SECURITIES, NEW YORK:

“We have a mixed bag of macro news here. Of course, the downward revision of GDP was much more than expected and that's not good news, along with the fact that consumer spending was revised downward.

"The good news is that the inflation data measured by the PCE basically in line with expectations. ... Inflation remains elevated, sticky and with the possibility of energy prices eventually moving into the pipeline, the Fed is likely to stay on hold for a longer period of time.”

TIM GHRISKEY, SENIOR PORTFOLIO STRATEGIST, INGALLS & SNYDER, NEW YORK:

"Most of today's economic numbers were generally in line with expectations with the exception of durable goods orders, which was weak and the GDP estimate which was also weak. There's some concern about the economy from these numbers. These are numbers worth looking at and they question the strength of the U.S. economy. War issues in the Middle East are the most important determinant of financial markets at the moment."

data.title: Gap Between Canada, U.S. Unemployment Rates Narrows Despite Canada's Jump in February — Market Talk
data.description: Statistics Canada says the country's unemployment rate rose to 6.7% in February, up from 6.5% in the prior month. When calculated using US Department of Labor methodology, Canada's unemployment rate in February was 5.6%, or unchanged from 13 months ago. The US unemployment rate was 4.4% in February, up from 4% in January, 2025. The gap between the Canadian and US unemployment rates narrowed over that time period, Statistics Canada says. This points to weaker labor-market conditions in the U.S. versus Canada since the Trump administration shifted trade policy toward a more protectionist bent. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

data.title: Canada Employment Drops in February, Lifting Jobless Rate to 6.7% — Update
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

OTTAWA--Employers in Canada for a second straight month shed jobs, partially rolling back a jump in hiring seen last fall and pushing the jobless rate back up following a dip to start the year.

A net 83,900 jobs were lost last month, the most since the first month of 2009 outside of the height of the global pandemic, Statistics Canada said Friday.

That was much weaker than slight 10,000 gain in employment economists were expecting, and with more people searching for work the unemployment rate pushed up 0.2 percentage point to 6.7%.

It comes after a more modest 24,800 jobs were cut in January, though the jobless rate remains roughly where it was in February 2025, and below the 6.8% seen in December and recent high of 7.1% logged last August and September.

"This is clearly a very worrisome report for the Bank of Canada that shows that labor market slack has increased and activity is frozen amidst trade uncertainty," Katherine Judge, senior economist at CIBC Capital Markets, said.

When calculated using U.S. Labor Department methodology, Canada's unemployment rate was 0.2 point higher at 5.6% and steady on where it stood at the start of 2025. In contrast, the unemployment rate in the U.S. has edged higher, hitting 4.4% in February, up 0.4 point from January 2025.

Only sluggish growth is expected from Canada's economy this year as trade uncertainty continues to weigh and after the federal government has taken steps to curb immigration. The Bank of Canada, which meets next week to decide on interest rates, has projected the economy will grow just 1.8% annualized in the first quarter and a soft 1.1% over 2026.

The size of Canada's labor force shrank by 27,200 during February, building on the 119,000 fall to start the year. It comes as the population has remained largely unchanged in the new year, after the federal government moved to cut back on once booming immigration.

The fall in employment in Canada was concentrated in full-time work and for a second consecutive month among private sector workers.

In all, full-time roles dropped by 108,400, more than offsetting the 24,500 gain in part-time work. Though little changed on 12 months earlier, the number of employees in the private sector was down 0.5% from the month before, whereas the numbers in the public sector and among the self-employed were little changed.

The participation rate--the proportion of the working-age population who were either employed or unemployed--dipped 0.1 point to 64.9% in February.

At the same time, the employment rate, or the proportion of the working-age population that is employed, slipped to 60.6% from 60.8%.

Wage growth picked up in the latest month, and remains well above consumer price inflation that has hovered around the central bank's 2% target for about two years. Wages for permanent employees climbed 4.2% on a year earlier, accelerating from 3.3% growth the month before and outpacing the 3.2% advance economists expected.

Data this week from jobs site Indeed indicated Canadians were back hunting for jobs as the new year started, with searches and applications rising 19% in January following a drop the month before--a similar seasonal pattern to that seen in 2025. Still, Indeed said job postings in mid-February were flat from a year earlier, with a pickup for sectors such as retail and technology countering softening of demand in healthcare and education.

Write to Robb M. Stewart robb.stewart@wsj.com

At 9:31 a.m. ET, the S&P/TSX composite index was up 0.41% at 32,957.26 points.

data.title: Adobe to pay $75 million to resolve US lawsuit over fees, subscription cancellations
data.description:
Adobe said on Friday it will pay $75 million to resolve a U.S. government lawsuit accusing the Photoshop and Acrobat maker of harming consumers by concealing hefty termination fees and making it difficult to cancel subscriptions.

The San Jose, California-based company said it will also provide $75 million of free services to customers, in addition to making the $75 million payment to the U.S. Department of Justice.

Court approval is required, and Adobe denied wrongdoing in agreeing to settle.

In a June 2024 complaint , the Justice Department and Federal Trade Commission accused Adobe of burying termination fees that could reach hundreds of dollars in the fine print for its "annual paid monthly" subscription plan, or behind textboxes and hyperlinks.

They also said Adobe forced subscribers who wanted to cancel online to wade through numerous pages, and subscribers who wanted to cancel by phone to repeat themselves to multiple representatives and encounter "resistance and delay" from them.

data.title: Injective to Release AI Master Guide on March 13th
data.description:
Injective announced that its AI master guide will be released on March 13. According to the announcement, the publication will serve as a broad guide to AI within the Injective ecosystem.

The release is positioned as a new resource for users interested in AI-related development and activity on Injective.

Refer to the official tweet by INJ:

Injective 🥷
@injective

The largest master guide to AI on Injective drops tomorrow. 👀

Notifications on. 🔔

Mar 12, 2026

INJ Info

Injective Protocol is a decentralized exchange (DEX) protocol built on the Cosmos SDK, offering advanced cross-chain trading capabilities.

Key features of Injective Protocol include:

1. Decentralization: On the Injective platform, users have full control over their funds, with no third-party intervention.

2. Cross-chain trading: Injective supports trading between different blockchains, providing users with a wide range of tradable assets.

3. High performance: Injective utilizes the Tendermint consensus system, ensuring high throughput and low transaction latency.

4. Gas-free transactions: Users can trade without the need to pay gas fees.

INJ is the native token of Injective Protocol. The token is used for protocol governance, staking, and participation in voting processes that address key protocol upgrades and management decisions.

data.title: Ballard Power Shifts to Revenue Ramp After Structural Reset — Market Talk
data.description: Ballard Power Systems has finished its structural reset and is now in the revenue ramp-up phase. Jefferies analyst Dushyant Ailani says in a report that with 4Q gross margin turning positive and cash operating expenses down 41% year-over-year, the cost-base is largely right-sized, making 2026 a "run the playbook" year. The focus shifts to revenue growth across bus, stationary, rail, and material handling, supported by tighter commercial terms and systematic product cost-downs. The New Flyer contract is an example of "improved commercial discipline," but Ailani notes that revenue timing remains unclear, with some announcements pushed into 2026. Guidance for a 40/60 revenue split in 1H/2H suggests a back-half weighed ramp. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

data.title: South African rand set for second weekly loss as oil surge stokes inflation fears
data.description:
The South African rand extended its fall on Friday, heading for a second consecutive week of losses as surging energy prices fuelled by conflict in the Middle East rattled global markets and heightened inflation concerns.

  • At 1320 GMT the rand traded at 16.80 against the dollar , little changed from its Thursday close of 16.7950, while the greenback was last up 0.4% against a basket of currencies.

  • The rand has faced sustained pressure since the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran started nearly two weeks ago. It fell more than 3% last week and is poised to decline by around 2% this week. Rising oil prices pose a challenge for South Africa, a net energy importer.

  • Oil prices rose above $100 a barrel, despite the U.S. trying to ease supply concerns by issuing a 30-day licence for countries to buy Russian oil and petroleum products stranded at sea.

  • "While this war continues and the stress in the oil market builds, one should expect emerging market currencies such as the rand and its LATAM peers to underperform developed-market currencies, especially the U.S. dollar," ETM Analytics said.

  • On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index fell 0.6%.

  • South Africa's benchmark 2035 government bond (ZAR2035=) also weakened, as the yield rose 1.7 basis points to 8.885%.

  • Next week, domestically focused investors will look to the country's statistics agency's release of inflation (ZACPIY=ECI) and retail sales (ZARET=ECI) data.

  • Investec economist Lara Hodes said inflation was forecast at 0.5% m/m and 3.1% y/y for February, from 3.5% y/y previously.

  • "Owing to the persistent war in the Middle East, a cut in interest rates by the SARB in March is now not anticipated with elevated oil prices and a depreciating rand an upside risk to the inflation outlook," said Hodes.

data.title: Bancor Network to Participate in EthCC in Cannes on April 1st
data.description:
Bancor Network announced that the Token Engineering Academy Breakout Session will take place on April 1 at the main venue of EthCC in Cannes. According to the announcement, the session is designed as a hands-on workshop for researchers, protocol designers, engineers, and economists working on decentralized systems.

The event will focus on mechanism and system design in the context of real decentralized systems.

Refer to the official tweet by BNT:

Bancor
@Bancor

Introducing the @tokengineering Breakout Session â€“ a hands-on mechanism and system design workshop for researchers, protocol designers, engineers, and economists working on real decentralized systems.

Taking place April 1st at the main venue of @EthCC[9] in Cannes 🇫🇷

Hosted inâ€¦

Mar 12, 2026

BNT Info

Bancor is a decentralized finance (DeFi) protocol that focuses on liquidity provision and automated token trading, enabling seamless cross-chain conversions without relying on traditional centralized exchanges. It leverages blockchain technology to facilitate direct and secure token trading.

Bancor’s core protocol, Carbon, introduces a decentralized trading system with on-chain limit and range orders, allowing users to implement automated trading strategies like “buy low, sell high”. Once executed, these orders cannot be reversed, providing security against market manipulation, such as MEV sandwich attacks. Additionally, users can adjust their orders directly on the blockchain.

The native token, BNT, is used for staking and governance through BancorDAO, where the community makes decisions regarding the development and operations of the Bancor ecosystem. BNT also supports liquidity provision and governance within the protocol.

data.title: KALENDER-Termine Politik/Vermischtes bis 22. März
data.description:
*************************************************************

Weitere ausführliche Terminübersichten erhalten sie unter:

* FIRMEN/MÄRKTE

* KONJUNKTUR/GELDPOLITIK

*************************************************************

Alle Zeitangaben in Central European Time (CET)

SATURDAY MARCH 14, 2026

01:00 - The inaugural Indo-Pacific Energy Security Ministerial and Business Forum - U.S. President Donald Trump's "National Energy Dominance Council (NEDC)" hosts the inaugural Indo-Pacific Energy Security Ministerial and Business Forum with Japan in Tokyo on March 14 and 15.

11:00 - Belarusian Kalesnikava receives belated her 2022 Karlspreis - Belarusian opposition leader Maria Kalesnikava receives belated her originally 2022 awarded International Charlemagne Prize (Karlspreis) in Aachen

14:30 - Iranians in German exile protest for regime change in home country - Several thousand Iranians in German exile and their supporters are expected to protest in Hamburg for a regime change in their home country.

SUNDAY MARCH 15, 2026

Congo Republic to hold presidential election - Congo Republic is expected to hold a presidential election. President Denis Sassou Nguesso who came to power in 1979 is allowed to participate in the poll.

First round of municipal elections in France - French people vote in the first round of mayoral elections in France as the far right National Rally hopes to position itself as the dominant force ahead of the 2027 presidential elections by taking control of major cities in France.

North Korea holds Supreme People's Assembly election - North Korea will elect new representatives to its rubber-stamp legislature, the Supreme People's Assembly, on March 15

Vietnam holds parliamentary election - Tens of millions of Vietnamese, including Communist Party chief To Lam, go to the polls on Sunday to elect 500 members of the National Assembly.

01:00 - Second day of Indo-Pacific Energy Security Ministerial and Business Forum - U.S. President Donald Trump's "National Energy Dominance Council(NEDC)" hosts the inaugural Indo-Pacific Energy Security Ministerial and Business Forum with Japan in Tokyo on March 14 and 15.

10:30 - Orban and Magyar hold political speeches and rallies on Hungarian revolution anniversary - Opposition politician Peter Magyar to hold a big rally, while Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban will hold a speech as Hungary celebrates the anniversary of the 1848-1849 Hungarian revolution against Austrian rule.

10:30 - Nordic and Canadian leaders gather in Oslo - Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Stoere hosts a Nordic-Canadian summit in Oslo. Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney and prime ministers of Denmark, Finland, Iceland and Sweden will participate.

MONDAY MARCH 16, 2026

Jury selection for former US Congressman's illegal Venezuela lobbying trial - Former U.S. Congressman David Rivera faces trial in Miami federal court for allegedly illegally acting as an agent of former Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro's government. No text coverage is expected for jury selection, which is scheduled to begin on March 16. Opening statements and trial testimony due to begin on March 23. 08:00 - EU energy and foreign affairs ministers meet in Brussels as prices rise over Iran war

EU countries' energy and foreign affairs ministers gather in Brussels for a regular meeting, which is expected to be dominated by surging energy prices due to the war in Iran.

11:40 - German, Estonian defense ministers meet in Berlin - German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius will host his Latvian counterpart Andris Spruds for his inaugural visit in Berlin. The meeting will focus on security policy, NATO's eastern flank, support for Ukraine and bilateral defense cooperation.

TUESDAY MARCH 17, 2026

EU climate ministers meet in Brussels to discuss ETS changes - European Union countries' climate ministers meet in Brussels to discuss upcoming climate change policies, including a planned review of the bloc's emissoins trading system (ETS). Illinois Democrats fight for retiring Senate veteran Durbin's seat

09:15 - Three Ukrainians accused of espionage for Russia go on trial in Germany - Three Ukrainian nationals stand trial in the southwestern German city of Stuttgart for an alleged conspiracy in relation to arson attacks targeting western freighter jets and engaging in espionage activities on behalf of Russian intelligence. Russia has denied any involvement.

16:30 - US judge to weigh renewed bid to stop Trump ballroom project - A judge in Washington will weigh a challenge to President Donald Trump's authority to proceed with the construction of a $400-million White House ballroom on the site of the demolished East Wing. US District Judge Richard Leon declined earlier to issue a preliminary injunction but gave the plaintiff, National Trust for Historic Preservation, a second change to argue the president is acting outside his powers.

18:00 - UN Security Council discusses hostilities in the Middle East - The United Nations Security Council meets for consultations on hostilities between Hezbollah in Lebanon and Israel.

19:00 - Trump administration intelligence officials testify in US Congress - Top Trump administration intelligence officials testify to the House intelligence committee at their annual hearing looking at worldwide threats to the United States. Hearing is expected to focus largely on security implications of the Iran war.

WEDNESDAY MARCH 18, 2026

Dutch vote in municipal election month after government installation - Nationwide municipal elections in the Netherlands provide a first test of the support for the center right minority government installed on Feb 23. COVERAGE ON MERIT

10:00 - German government holds cabinet meeting in Berlin

13:00 - German police forces and the armed forces take part in the counterterrorism exercise - German federal police, police forces and the armed forces Bundeswehr take part in the counterterrorism exercise GETEX 2026

14:00 - German Chancellor Friedrich Merz gives a government statement on the European Council at the lower house of parliament Bundestag in Berlin

14:30 - Trump homeland nominee testifies at US Senate confirmation hearing - U.S. President Donald Trump's nominee for homeland security secretary, U.S. Senator Markwayne Mullin, will testify on March 18 at a U.S. Senate panel confirmation hearing.

15:00 - Trump administration intelligence officials testify in US Congress - Top Trump administration intelligence officials testify to the U.S. Senate and House intelligence committees at their annual hearings looking at worldwide threats to the United States. Hearings are expected to focus largely on security implications of the Iran war.

19:00 - United States-Fed policy decision

THURSDAY MARCH 19, 2026

EU leaders’ summit takes place in Brussels - EU leaders meet in Brussels for a two-day summit, set to discuss soaring energy prices and how to mitigate them, amid the U.S.-Iran war.

Japan's Takaichi expected to meet with Trump - Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is expected to meet with U.S. President Donald Trump in Washington.

FRIDAY MARCH 20, 2026

EU leaders’ summit takes place in Brussels - EU leaders meet in Brussels for a two-day summit, set to discuss soaring energy prices and how to mitigate them, amid the U.S.-Iran war.

17:30 - German Chancellor Merz attends the final election campaign event of Rhineland-Palatinate CDU - German Chancellor Friedrich Merz attends the final election campaign event of Rhineland-Palatinate's Christian Democratic Union (CDU) top candidate Gordon Schnieder for upcomming state elections

SATURDAY MARCH 21, 2026

NO EVENTS SCHEDULED

SUNDAY MARCH 22, 2026

NO EVENTS SCHEDULED
